title: Definition of Al-Mafʿul Maʿah (the Object of Accompaniment)

sections.text: Consider the following sentences:

items.text: — I traveled along with the dawn (i.e., at dawn).
— The hunter goes out along with the rising of the sun.
— Rashid came forward, smiling, along with his horse.

sections.text: What does reflection on the underlined nouns (wa-al-fajra, wa-tuluʿa, wa-hisanahu) tell us? It clearly shows that they are nouns in the accusative case (mansub) and that they fall after a 'waw' (the letter و). What does that waw mean here? It carries the sense of 'with' (maʿa).
The waw that carries this meaning is called waw al-maʿiyya ('the waw of accompaniment'), and the noun put in the accusative after it is called: al-mafʿul maʿah ('the object of accompaniment').
Al-mafʿul maʿah (the object of accompaniment) is a noun in the accusative case mentioned after a waw that carries the meaning of 'with' — that waw being called waw al-maʿiyya.
Sentences About Al-Mafʿul Maʿah

items.text: — Leave me alone with the book.
— The eagle is flying alongside the cloud.
— Easy on the patient! (lit. 'go gently along with the patient')
— March along with your brothers, united.
— What concern is it of yours along with the affairs of others? (i.e., 'what business have you with...?')

sections.text: Conditions for Al-Mafʿul Maʿah
1 — It must be preceded by a complete sentence; it can never be preceded by [just] a noun. And it is joined to a waw meaning 'with'.

items.text: — What [business have] you along with Sulayman?

sections.text: 2 — Al-mafʿul maʿah cannot precede its sentence. So we cannot say:

items.text: — *'And [along with] the mountain, Ali walked' (incorrect ordering).

sections.text: Iʿrab of Al-Mafʿul Maʿah
The ruling for al-mafʿul maʿah is the accusative (al-nasb). However, al-mafʿul maʿah can be confused with al-ʿatf (conjunction). The distinction between them is as follows:
1 — If conjoining (ʿatf) makes no sense semantically but accompaniment (maʿiyya) does, the noun after the waw is al-mafʿul maʿah, e.g.:

items.text: — I walked along (with the route alongside) the mountain.

sections.text: Conjunction does not make sense — because the mountain does not walk; accompaniment does make sense. So (al-jabal) is al-mafʿul maʿah in the accusative.
2 — If accompaniment makes no sense but conjoining does, the noun after the waw is conjoined (maʿtuf ʿalayh), e.g.:

items.text: — I visited Mecca and [then] al-Madinah.

sections.text: Accompaniment does not make sense — because I visited Mecca first, then visited al-Madinah; so 'al-Madinah' is conjoined onto 'Makka'.
Examples of Iʿrab for Al-Mafʿul Maʿah

items.text: — The water became level along with the plank (i.e., reached its level).

sections.text:
istawa: a past-tense verb fixed on an estimated fatha at its end, prevented from appearing because of taʿadhdhur (the alif cannot bear a vowel).
al-maʾu: the subject (faʿil) in the nominative case, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
wa-al-khashabata: the waw — waw al-maʿiyya; al-khashabata — mafʿul maʿah in the accusative case, marked by the manifest fatha at its end.

items.text: — Zayd is walking along the road.

sections.text:
Zayd: mubtadaʾ in the nominative case, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
saʾir: khabar (an active participle, ism al-faʿil) in the nominative case, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
wa-al-tariqa: the waw — waw al-maʿiyya; al-tariq — mafʿul maʿah in the accusative case, marked by the manifest fatha at its end.

items.text: — How are you along with [coping with] the exam?

sections.text:
kayfa: an interrogative noun fixed on fatha, in the position of nominative as a fronted khabar.
anta: a separate pronoun fixed on fatha, in the position of nominative as a postponed mubtadaʾ.
wa-al-imtihana: the waw — waw al-maʿiyya; al-imtihan — mafʿul maʿah in the accusative case, marked by the manifest fatha at its end.
Examples of Al-Mafʿul Maʿah from the Noble Quran
Allah Most High said:

items.text: — "...so resolve upon your plan and [call upon] your associates..." — Quran 10:71 (Sahih International)
— "And [also for] those who were settled in the Home [i.e., al-Madinah] and [adopted] the faith before them..." — Quran 59:9 (Sahih International)

sections.text: It is also permissible to take the waw in these two verses as a conjunction, with what follows it being a direct object of an omitted verb — implied as 'invoke and gather' (idʿu wa-jmaʿu) in the first verse, and 'be sincere [in faith]' (akhlasu) in the second.
Exercises on Al-Mafʿul Maʿah
1 — Place each of the following nouns as al-mafʿul maʿah in a meaningful sentence.

items.text: the mountain
the shore
the friend
the morning
the field
the sea

sections.text: 2 — Distinguish waw al-maʿiyya from other instances of waw in the following sentences:

items.text: — I accompanied my father and my mother on the journey.
— I awoke along with the sunrise.
— My sister slept along with her doll.

items.transliteration: ittafaqa akhi wa-sadiq-u-hu ʿala al-dhahab-i ila al-sinima
items.text: — My brother and his friend agreed to go to the cinema.

sections.text: 3 — Provide the iʿrab for the underlined portions:

items.text: — Leave me alone with my own affairs.
— I walked along the irrigation canal.
— What [business have] you along with your friend Saʿid?

sections.text: Try working through the answers yourself first, then check them against a teacher or grammar reference.
